name: AWE HPC
oneLiner: A largely undisclosed national-security HPC programme that still matters to the UK's overall public compute picture.
summary: The Atomic Weapons Establishment operates one of the UK's most powerful high-performance computing programmes for national security and safety analysis. Public technical details are limited, but the UK compute roadmap explicitly includes AWE's HPC capability in the wider public research and innovation compute ecosystem. Its relevance here is strategic rather than open access: it demonstrates the depth of sovereign UK capability even though usage is restricted.

keyFacts.performance: One of the UK's most powerful HPC programmes according to public roadmap context

access.route: Access is restricted to authorised national-security and safety-analysis work.

useCases: Specialised AI algorithm research in restricted domains
